name: Puunui
desc: Quiet residential area above Nuuanu near Oahu Country Club — shady streets, mature trees, peaceful and overlooked.
insider: Oahu Country Club borders the neighborhood — residents get lush green space views without paying membership fees
The streets are wide and shady with mature monkeypod trees — one of the best neighborhoods for evening walks in Honolulu
Sandwiched between Nuuanu and Pacific Heights, Puunui gets the valley lushness and the ridge breezes — best of both worlds
Very little through traffic — most people driving here actually live here, which keeps it remarkably quiet
Puunui is home to the Japanese Consulate and several diplomatic residences — the manicured properties add to the neighborhood charm
The old Puunui homes from the 1940s-60s have a classic Honolulu mid-century character — jalousie windows and covered lanais
Wylie Street connects down to Liliha — a quick 5-minute drive to Liliha Bakery for coco puffs
The elevation provides noticeably cooler air than downtown below — a genuine comfort in summer months
Homes are more affordable than adjacent Pacific Heights — similar quiet character without the extreme price tag
The Royal Mausoleum is just down the hill in Nuuanu — one of the most significant Hawaiian cultural sites on Oahu
